A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE TOWN OF EUBANK ON THE AFTERNOON OF APRIL 10TH. THE TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN ON ELLISON-PULASKI ROAD...NEAR HIGHWAY 70. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ONE HALF MILE IN PULASKI COUNTY...BEFORE EXITING THE COUNTY. MOST OF THE DAMAGE CAUSED BY THE TORNADO WAS TO TREES...WITH A COUPLE OF SHINGLES ALSO PEELED OFF A BARN ROOF. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AN EF0 ON THE ENHANCED FUJITA SCALE...WITH WINDS UP TO 85 MPH.
